svg.title: SinLess Games 24U Rack Professional Front and Rear Plan
svg.desc: A professional 24U rack plan showing front and rear rack faces, UniFi networking, rear 10Gb SFP+ backbone, five 2U Dell R710 servers, 4U disk shelf, 3U UPS, patch panel, Raspberry Pi shelf, power distribution, and cable routing.
svg.text: Front + rear layout with rear 10Gb SFP+ backbone, dual power distribution, 5x 2U R710 nodes, 4U disk shelf, 3U UPS, UniFi network upgrade path

g.text: 2U front patching field / short patch leads to access switch
1U Cable Manager / Rear Backbone Service Gap
4x Raspberry Pi Shelf / Shallow Front Mount
2U / owned / dual 10Gb SFP+ to rear backbone
2U / owned / dual 10Gb SFP+ to rear backbone
2U / planned / dual 10Gb SFP+ to rear backbone
2U / planned / dual 10Gb SFP+ to rear backbone
2U / planned / dual 10Gb SFP+ to rear backbone
4U SAS Disk Shelf / HBA Storage Shelf
Ready rails / rear SAS + dual power / storage expansion
Heavy equipment at bottom / feeds rear 0U PDUs
Structured cabling exits toward walls / desks / AP drops
Rear of Pro Max 48 PoE / Power + Uplinks
Gateway Rear IO / WAN + Core SFP+ Uplinks
Dual SAS paths to HBA host / dual PSU to PDU A+B
Feeds 0U PDU A + 0U PDU B / battery-backed rack power

svg.text: 1. Heavy equipment stays low: UPS at U3-U1, disk shelf at U7-U4.
2. Network equipment stays high: patching, PoE access, gateway, and SFP+ aggregation at U24-U19.
3. 10Gb SFP+ backbone is rear-facing so server DACs stay short and cable-managed.
4. Raspberry Pi shelf is shallow front-mounted; rear U18 can hold the existing Pyle PDU as service power.
External SAS HBA paths to disk shelf
iDRAC / management RJ45 to patch panel
